question.text: Which of the following is a unique benefit of Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGBs) compared to physical gold and gold ETFs?

answer.text: SGBs provide exposure to international gold prices
SGBs pay 2.5% per annum interest semi-annually in addition to gold price appreciation
SGBs can be redeemed at any time without restriction
SGBs are exempt from GST like physical gold

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Sovereign Gold Bonds uniquely offer 2.5% per annum interest paid semi-annually, in addition to any capital appreciation linked to gold prices. Neither physical gold nor gold ETFs provide any periodic income. Additionally, SGBs have no GST (unlike physical gold which attracts GST), no purity concerns, and no storage costs. **The Trap:** Physical gold actually attracts GST (3% on purchase), so the statement that SGBs are "exempt from GST like physical gold" is doubly wrong. SGBs have a 5-year lock-in with 8-year maturity, so they cannot be redeemed "at any time." **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers gold funds and ETFs; NISM Series X-A tests gold investment alternatives including SGBs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 2.5% annual interest on SGBs is a powerful differentiator -- over an 8-year holding period, the cumulative interest of 20% significantly enhances total returns compared to physical gold or gold ETFs, plus capital gains are tax-free if held to maturity with RBI.
